task.task_description: This is a systematic electromagnetic calorimeter performance study that investigates the relationship between detector depth and shower containment efficiency. The task involves conducting Monte Carlo simulations using Geant4 to model electromagnetic showers produced by electrons in a homogeneous lead tungstate (PbWO4) calorimeter. The study requires testing three different calorimeter depths (16, 20, and 25 radiation lengths) across multiple beam energies to quantify energy containment, resolution, and shower development characteristics. The investigation aims to establish design criteria for calorimeter depth by measuring energy leakage and determining the minimum thickness needed to achieve specific containment performance targets.
task.goal: Determine the minimum calorimeter depth required to keep energy leakage below 1% at 20 GeV and characterize how calorimeter depth affects electromagnetic shower containment across different energies

constraints.description: Energy leakage must be below 1% at 20 GeV
Use homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter material only
Run one depth configuration per geometry call

task.context:
Investigate how calorimeter depth affects electromagnetic shower containment.

**STUDY DESIGN:**
Use a homogeneous PbWO4 (lead tungstate) calorimeter block.
Test multiple calorimeter depths by varying the number of layers or total thickness.

Target depths to compare:
- 16 radiation lengths (X₀ of PbWO4 = 0.89 cm, so ~14.2 cm)
- 20 radiation lengths (~17.8 cm)
- 25 radiation lengths (~22.3 cm)

**GEOMETRY OPTIONS:**
- "box" or "homogeneous_slab" topology with PbWO4 as active material
- Vary the active_thickness parameter to set different depths

**PRACTICAL CONSTRAINTS (for tractable simulation):**
- Energy sweep: use particle_energy: [1, 5, 20] (GeV)
- Use e- (electrons) in Geant4 particle gun
- Run one depth configuration per geometry call

**MEASURE at each depth and energy:**
1. Fraction of incident energy deposited in the calorimeter (containment)
2. Energy resolution (σ_E / E)
3. Position of shower maximum (depth of peak energy deposition from hit z-positions)

**GOAL:**
Determine the minimum calorimeter depth required to keep energy leakage below 1% at 20 GeV.
Plot containment fraction vs depth for each energy.

Test 3 configurations (one per depth).

steps.name: generate_geometry_16X0
steps.description: Generate GDML geometry for homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter with 16 radiation lengths depth (~14.2 cm)

steps.name: simulate_electrons_16X0
steps.description: Run Geant4 simulation with electron beam at multiple energies for 16 X0 depth calorimeter

steps.name: generate_geometry_20X0
steps.description: Generate GDML geometry for homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter with 20 radiation lengths depth (~17.8 cm)

steps.name: simulate_electrons_20X0
steps.description: Run Geant4 simulation with electron beam at multiple energies for 20 X0 depth calorimeter

steps.name: generate_geometry_25X0
steps.description: Generate GDML geometry for homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter with 25 radiation lengths depth (~22.3 cm)

steps.name: simulate_electrons_25X0
steps.description: Run Geant4 simulation with electron beam at multiple energies for 25 X0 depth calorimeter

steps.name: analyze_containment
steps.description: Analyze energy containment fraction, resolution, and shower maximum position for all configurations

steps.name: plot_containment_results
steps.description: Create plots showing containment fraction vs depth for each energy, and determine minimum depth for <1% leakage at 20 GeV

steps.name: generate_final_report
steps.description: Synthesize all results into comprehensive report on calorimeter depth optimization

plan.reasoning: This workflow systematically tests three calorimeter depths with energy sweeps to efficiently determine containment performance. The parallel energy sweeps minimize simulation time while providing comprehensive data for analysis. The analysis extracts key metrics (containment, resolution, shower max) needed to determine the minimum depth for <1% energy leakage at 20 GeV.
